Standard Audiopaths

If your application does not play exclusively on audiopaths created from audiopath configuration objects, you must create one or more standard audiopaths.

Standard audiopaths are identified by the values passed in the dwType parameter of IDirectMusicPerformance8::CreateStandardAudioPath or in the dwDefaultPathType parameter of IDirectMusicPerformance8::InitAudioX.

The audiopaths defined by DirectMusic manage the flow of synthesizer output through combinations of standard buffers. The following table lists the standard audiopaths, and describes whether or not the audio data is sent directly to a mixbin. Audiopaths not sent directly to one or more mixbins utilize an additional DirectSound buffer for submixing. In addition to mono voices, some audiopaths support stereo, four channel and six channel waves. The table also shows how many channels a source wave may contain.

Audiopath Type Uses an additional mixin buffer? Wave channels supported
DMUS_APATH_DYNAMIC_3D Yes 1
DMUS_APATH_DYNAMIC_MONO Yes 1
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_5DOT1 No 1, 2, 6
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_5DOT1_ENV No 1
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_5DOT1_MUSIC No 1, 2, 4
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_QUAD No 1, 2, 4
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_QUAD_ENV No 1
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_QUAD_MUSIC No 1, 2, 6
DMUS_APATH_MIXBIN_STEREO_EFFECTS No 1, 2, 4
DMUS_APATH_SHARED_STEREO No 1, 2
DMUS_APATH_SHARED_STEREOPLUSREVERB No 1, 2

IDirectSoundBuffer8 is available for any audiopaths that use an additional DirectSound buffer (in other words, it does not send its content directly to a mixbin). For example, you would be able to access a IDirectSoundBuffer8 interface on a DMUS_APATH_DYNAMIC_MONO audiopath, but not on a DMUS_APATH_SHARED_STEREO audiopath.

To obtain an interface to the DirectSound buffer for types DMUS_APATH_DYNAMIC_3D and DMUS_APATH_DYNAMIC_MONO, call GetObjectInPath with dwStage set to DMUS_PATH_BUFFER and dwBuffer set to 0.

For more information on obtaining interfaces, see Retrieving Objects from an Audiopath.
